name: Pipeline Upheaval Buckling
expected_response_detailed: Upheaval in buried pipelines: thermal expansion with vertical restraint creates compressive axial load. Critical condition when axial force exceeds >4EI/L² (Euler buckling). Prevention: deeper burial, trench backfill quality, intermittent rock dump (virtual anchor), vertical loops for expansion, on-bottom stability for offshore. Design: Hobbs upheaval formula, vertical out-of-straightness limit 0.5% typically, effective axial force calculation including temperature and pressure effects.

name: Cold Weather Pipeline Operation
expected_response_detailed: Low temperature concerns: brittle fracture susceptibility, decompression limits per CSA Z662, Joule-Thomson cooling (-7°F per 1,000 psi drop), hydrate formation (temperature above hydrate curve required), viscosity increase for liquids. Materials: Charpy V-notch impact testing temperature, transition curve shift. Protection: methanol/glycol injection for hydrate inhibition, insulation on above-ground pipe, heat tracing for instrumentation, freeze protection for valves and instruments.

name: Inertial Pigging Design
expected_response_detailed: Inertial pig (foam or unsealed) for dewaxing: velocity 5-15 ft/sec, bypass ports to prevent stuck conditions, cup design for scraping, magnet for MFL tools, transmitter for tracking. Launcher design for inertial pigs (larger bore than pipeline). Velocity control via back pressure. Cleaning efficiency 70-90% typically. Multiple pigs in train for heavy deposits. Barrels 2× pipe length minimum.

name: Hydrotest Water Treatment
expected_response_detailed: Hydrotest water specification: pH 5-8, chlorides <50 ppm for stainless, suspended solids <50 ppm, oxygen <1 ppm if stainless present. Treatment: filtration, pH adjustment with sodium carbonate, biocide addition if hold >3 days, corrosion inhibitor 200-500 ppm for carbon steel. Disposal: settlement ponds, pH adjustment, discharge permit compliance, NPDES or state specific. Drying: air displacement with methanol slug, nitrogen displacement, vacuum drying.

name: Pipeline Capacity Optimization
expected_response_detailed: Capacity increase options: loop lines (parallel segment), line pack utilization (pressure increase), drag reduction agents (0.5-2% throughput increase, ongoing cost), compressor station addition (incremental), dehydration bypass (if specification allows), MAOP increase (if test data supports). Economic analysis: incremental cost vs revenue, net present value, payback period. Constraints: class location limiting MAOP, compression power availability.

name: Vortex Shedding Analysis
expected_response_detailed: Helmholtz resonator or vortex shedding in thermowells and probes: Strouhal number S = f×D/V, critical velocity when shedding frequency matches natural frequency of structure. Prevention: helical strakes, shrouds, increased stiffness, reduced length/diameter ratio, turbulence promotion. Piping: Spans susceptible if f_shedding ≈ f_span. Damping via snubbers. Thermowell design: ASME PTC 19.3 TW for wake frequency calculation.

name: Pipeline River Crossing Hydrology
expected_response_detailed: 100-year flood elevation determination: FEMA maps or site-specific analysis. Scour depth calculation: CSU or HEC-18 methods, typically 2-3 feet in cohesive soils, 5-10+ in non-cohesive. Countermeasures: riprap, articulating concrete blocks, sheet piling, guide banks. Depth of burial: below predicted scour depth plus 2 feet minimum. Directional drill preferred over open cut in flood-prone areas. Monitoring: settlement gauges after construction.

name: Facility Siting Per EP Guidelines
expected_response_detailed: NFPA 59A for LNG, OSHA PSM for high hazard. Siting: occupied building distances, property line clearances, fire protection water supply, access for emergency response, prevailing wind for vapor dispersion. Blast radius: from maximum credible event, overpressure 0.5 psi for buildings. Toxic release: ERPG-2 concentration at property line. Radiation: 5,000 Btu/ft²/hr at property line. Quantitative Risk Assessment for optimized siting.

name: Pipeline Integrity Management Plan
expected_response_detailed: IMP per 192 Subpart O: baseline assessment within 10 years, reassessment every 7 years maximum, five-year schedule, HCAs identified per 192.5, threats addressed (external corrosion, internal, SCC, third-party, material, equipment), prevention and mitigation measures, performance metrics, communication plan, management of change, quality assurance. Continuous integrity assessment vs periodic.

name: Meter Sizing Application
expected_response_detailed: Orifice meter: beta ratio 0.2-0.7 optimal (0.25-0.5 typical), permanent pressure loss 2-4× differential, turndown 3:1 typical, accuracy ±0.5% if calibrated range. Ultrasonic: path configuration (single-path 2-3% accuracy, multi-path 0.5%), straight run requirements (10D upstream, 5D downstream), turndown 100:1. Coriolis: accuracy 0.1-0.2%, liquid or dense gas only. Selection: fluid properties, flow range, accuracy requirement, maintenance access, cost lifecycle.
